title: NetConfig Pro Multi-Vendor Syntax & Compiler Metadata Schema
description: Authoritative machine-readable definitions for CLI compilation across 9 enterprise network operating systems.

rules.snoopingScope: MUST be placed strictly inside `ethernet-switching-options { secure-access-port { vlan {vlanName} { dhcp-snooping; arp-inspection; } } }`. Bare global secure-access-port dhcp-snooping is invalid and rejected on commit.
rules.dhcpTrustedPlacement: MUST be placed strictly under `secure-access-port { interface {aeX.0|ge-X/Y/Z.0} { dhcp-trusted; } }`. Never place under interface unit 0 family hierarchy.

vlans.namingConstraint: VLAN names must not be 'ip' or 'default' as they conflict with Junos reserved tokens.

interfaces.trunkMembersRule: Trunk unit 0 family ethernet-switching vlan members MUST explicitly list exact allowed VLAN names OR IDs plus the native VLAN (`members [ HR_NETWORK SERVER_FARM MGMT_VLAN ]`). Never rely solely on native-vlan-id.

routingProtocols.bgp: protocols { bgp { group {groupName} { type external; neighbor {ip} { peer-as {remoteAs}; } } } }

channelGroup.firmwareNote: Branch interface prefix based on channelMode: static aggregation uses sa{id}, while dynamic LACP (x510/x930/SBx series) uses po{id}.

exitAndSave: copy running-config working (Stage 1: Commit active changes to working storage)
copy working certified (Stage 2: Certify working configuration to survive chassis reboots)

productionSafeguards.featureSeparation: NEVER use `ip helper dhcp-snooping enable`. `ip helper` is exclusively for DHCP Relay across subnets. DHCP Snooping MUST use `dhcp-snooping admin-state enable` and `dhcp-snooping vlan {id} enable`.
productionSafeguards.vlanScopedArpInspection: Never output bare global `arp-inspection enable`. Must always be scoped: `arp-inspection vlan {id} enable` and trusted on uplinks via `arp-inspection port {chassis/slot/port} trust`.
productionSafeguards.chassisPortNumbering: 6860E/6900/9900 chassis use `1/1` (`slot/port`) or `1/1/1` (`chassis/slot/port`) format. Never output `0/1` as slot 0 does not host data ports. Verify exact naming via `show interfaces status`.
productionSafeguards.stormControlKeyword: Storm control uses `interfaces {port} bcast-storm threshold {val}`. Verify threshold keyword exact match against firmware minor release.
productionSafeguards.memoryPersistence: Do NOT use Cisco-style `write memory`. Exclusively save via explicit two-stage commit (`copy running-config working` AND `copy working certified`) so changes persist across chassis reboots.

l3Rules.releaseVersionNotes: AOS Release 8 initializes routing protocol contexts directly (`ip router ospf`, `ip router bgp`). Legacy AOS 6.x `ip load ospf` / `ip load bgp` commands are deprecated and omitted in AOS 8.

productionSafeguards.dhcpSnoopingTrust: Must explicitly trust uplink/trunk interfaces when DHCP snooping is enabled: `config dhcp_snooping ports {portNum} state enable trust`. Without this, legitimate DHCP server packets arriving on uplinks are dropped.
productionSafeguards.addressBindingPerPort: Global `enable address_binding dhcp_snoop` alone does not enforce IP-MAC inspection. Must pair with explicit port activation: `config address_binding dhcp_snoop ports {portNum} state enable`.
productionSafeguards.stormControlUnitVerification: Always verify traffic_control threshold units (`pps` vs `kbps`) against DGS firmware via `config traffic_control ?` before committing bare integer thresholds.
productionSafeguards.pvidMembershipAlignment: For native access/trunk VLANs, both `config ports {portNum} pvid {vid}` and `config vlan {vlanName} add untagged {portNum}` are generated to ensure cross-ASIC compatibility across older and newer D-Link switches.

staticRoute.standaloneConstraint: create iproute MUST ALWAYS include inline destination network and next hop (`create iproute 0.0.0.0/0 172.16.1.254 metric 1`). Bare toggles are invalid.
